question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a PublishRequest with sendRecord=true to force the inefficient 3-step path (receipt + cost query + record via TransactionRecordQuery) for every transaction, tripling per-transaction network round trips to processing nodes and raising node CPU/IO consumption above 30% compared to the baseline receipt-only path?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged user set both sendRecord=true and receipt=true in a PublishRequest to force the most expensive code path (TransactionRecordQuery with setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR))), causing the node to process a paid record query on top of the base transaction, increasing node resource consumption beyond the 30% threshold without sending additional transactions?
